title: Provider Capability Schema v1
description: Machine-verifiable structure for Provider/Model capability declarations. Derived from PROVIDER-CAPABILITY-CONTRACT v1.0 FINAL.

provenanceSource.description: Who provides the capability information. Ordered by trust: runtime_verification > provider_adapter > runtime_detection > external_catalog > config_hint > unknown. Config is not fact — config is just a hint.

evidenceType.description: Why we trust the capability value. api_response_observation = highest trust; static_config = lowest.

lifecycleState.description: Capability lifecycle state. unknown → detected → verified → deprecated. No bare 'true' allowed without a state chain.

provenanceObject.description: Provenance: who provides the capability information.

detail.description: Free-form detail about the provenance source, e.g. 'deepseek-provider-adapter v1.2' or 'detectOpenAICompletionsCompat() based on baseUrl=api.deepseek.com'

evidenceObject.description: Evidence: why we trust the capability value. Separate from provenance — provenance answers 'who says', evidence answers 'why believe'.

value.description: Specific observable fact supporting this capability, e.g. 'POST /chat/completions → response.reasoning_content present'

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this evidence was collected or verified

toolCallsSubCapability.description: Sub-capability: schema_validation strictness for tool_calls. Maps from ModelCompatConfig.supportsStrictMode.

format.description: Schema validation strictness. 'strict' = full JSON schema enforcement; 'lax' = permissive parsing.

capabilityEntry.description: A single capability entry. Every entry MUST have supported + provenance + evidence. Bare 'true' or 'false' without sources is forbidden.

supported.description: Whether the provider/model supports this capability. Not a preference, not a routing decision — a fact.

format.description: How the capability manifests. Separate from supported — format changes do NOT imply capability changes. Example: thinking.format = 'reasoning_content'. Must be null when supported = false.
format must be null when capability is not supported

capabilitySet.description: Set of capabilities for a single model. Each property name is a capability identifier. Extensible — future capabilities can be added without schema change.

propertyNames.description: Capability names must be lowercase snake_case, e.g. 'thinking', 'tool_calls', 'reasoning_effort'

modelEntry.description: Capability declaration for a single model.

inherits.description: Optional reference to a provider-level capability set from which this model inherits. When set, model-level capabilities override inherited ones.

provider.description: Provider identifier. Matches the key in openclaw.json models.providers, e.g. 'deepseek', 'ollama'.

capabilities.description: Provider-level default capabilities. Applied to all models unless overridden at model level.

additionalProperties.description: Future/unknown capabilities must still have provenance + evidence

models.description: Per-model capability overrides. Each key is a model id (e.g. 'deepseek-v4-flash'). Empty if all models use provider-level defaults.
